title: fix(langchain): Implement awrap_tool_call for async middleware

entities.name: awrap_tool_call
entities.description: An asynchronous wrapper function for tool calls, designed for middleware.

entities.name: async middleware
entities.description: A type of middleware designed to handle asynchronous operations.

entities.name: wrap_tool_call
entities.description: The synchronous version of the tool call wrapper.

entities.name: async function
entities.description: A function designed to execute asynchronously.

entities.name: handler
entities.description: A function or routine that processes requests or events, often part of a middleware chain.

entities.name: ToolMessage
entities.description: A specific message type used to convey tool-related information, including error details.

entities.name: Agent
entities.description: An autonomous software entity, likely an AI agent, that utilizes tools.

entities.name: Tool Error
entities.description: An error that occurs during the execution of a tool.

relations.label: USES
relations.evidence: Tested and verified with my own agent through the following snippet: @awrap_tool_call async def handle_tool_errors(request, handler):

relations.label: RELATED_TO
relations.evidence: return ToolMessage(content=f"Tool error: Please check your input and try again. ({str(e)})", tool_call_id=request.tool_call["id"], is_error=True)

extracted_data.summary: This issue describes the implementation of an asynchronous wrapper, `awrap_tool_call`, for middleware to handle async tool calls, which was verified with an agent.
